title: Level 241
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Cinzel+Decorative:wght@400;700;900&display=swap'); /* Page */ .page { border: solid 2px #00ffff; margin-top: 12px; position: relative; border-radius: 5px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 20px 0 #00ffff; } /* Darkmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(112,128,96,0.50); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(112,128,96,0.85); --theme-link-color: #0F3325; --theme-border-color: #00ffff; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px #06402b; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px rgba(10,10,10,1); } --theme-community-header-color: #0000ff); --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgba(0,255,255,0.7); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: #06402b; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: #06402b; } /* Lightmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(140, 160, 120, 1.0); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(140, 160, 120, 1.0); --theme-link-color: #0F3325; --theme-border-color: #00ffff; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px #06402b; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px rgba(10,10,10,1); } --theme-community-header-color: #0000ff); --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgba(0,255,255,0.7); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: #06402b; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: #06402b;.quicksand-<241font> font-family: "Quicksand", sans-serif; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; }}
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 241: "Winds of Change" }}{{Alt Class|env=2|ext=3|no=241|ent=2}}{{Notice|body=This page looks better in dark mode}}{{Blurbackground|file=SUNP0427.jpg}}
Level 241 is the 242nd abomination of the Backrooms.
{{Audio|file=22331 sleepcircle wind.ogg|loop=yes|secrettext=Wind sound effect (Credits to sleepCircle on freesound.org)|volume=1|round=yes}} {{Underline|FFFFFF}} == Description == {{Blurimage|file=SUNP0401.jpg|caption=An image of Level 241. The photographer is presumed dead.}} '''Level 241,''' also known as "'''Winds of Change,"''' is an endless expanse of grassy fields and rolling hillsThe current discovered area is approximately 11,500km3 with long stretches of barbed wire fences and roads scattered around the level at random. Large, white wind turbines are also present in large clusters, rotating at a slow speed. The turbines may differ in size, having longer or shorter blades, slightly changed color, or spinning faster than other turbines. Strangely, the turbines are able to spin without external stimulation such as wind or a power source. Small lakes and streams of [[Object 1|Almond water]] can also be found, typically being safe to consume. However, rare instances of the lakes being infected with the [[Hydrolitis Plague]] have been reported.


{{Blurimage|caption=Another image of Level 241. The image was found in [[Level 610]].|file=SUNP0409.jpg|align=left}}The [[XONE Laboratories|XONE]] has been keeping track of the state of '''Level 241'''. The scientists have categorized the conditions and events that occur within '''Level 241''' using a weather system that has been developed. The system covers and defines most wind-related events, such as thunderstorms and tornadoes, and provides instructions on how to avoid them.

Storms that form within '''Level 241''' are mildly anomalous. They are characterized by large, dark clouds with large bursts of rain falling beneath them. When one is approximately 3 kilometers away from these storms, one will experience a feeling of dread. The wind speed will gradually increase to 30 kph (18.6 mph) once one is on the border of the storm. Rain will also begin to fall within 30 meters of the storm. This anomalous storm forms tornadoes more commonly and quickly than other storms. The tornadoes will only move within the storm clouds, unable to devastate any area outside the vicinity of the storm clouds. Within the level, one may encounter a variety of objects, such as Almond Water or canned food and bottled water which are all safe to consume. == Areas & Structures == '''Level 241''' can be split into 4 unique areas, excluding plain fields. Though empty fields take up most of the space in the level, the following locations can be found within '''Level 241'''.
=== Highways: === Highways in '''Level 241''' run in a rough circular pattern about 100 kilometers from the center of the level. It is the most common location to find objects within the level. Supplies or objects are easily found in abandoned vehicles or by the side of the road. Rarely, cars can be found near highways. Unfortunately, the cars will remain stationary unless large amounts of fuel is provided to them. Although the cars seem to be in pristine condition, they are only able to be used in rare conditions.
{{Blurimage|file=City.jpg|caption=An image of the town, as viewed from a passing highway}} === The Town: === The town is located in the center of '''Level 241'''. It resembles a large cluster of abandoned buildings and streets surrounded on all sides by turbine fields and highways. Many resources, such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and [[Object 5|Greek Fire,]] can be found within the town. However, due to numerous threats such as aggressive entities and the poor structural integrity of the buildings, it is advised only to venture into the town to collect supplies and leave as soon as possible. In addition, gas stations and highway connections can be found near the town. The design and layout of the town may vary from time to time as the town [[Phenomenon 14|repairs itself]] from tornadoes or other damage in different formations.


=== Gas stations: === {{Blurimage|caption=An image of one of Level 241's gas stations. The image was found in a camera in [[Level 41]].|file=SUNP0754.jpg}} Gas stations can be found along the sides of '''Level 241'''’s highway networks. They usually contain no supplies. The gas pumps in the gas station are completely empty. Due to the lack of sustenance and materials in these places, wanderers usually use the gas stations as temporary shelters or a refuge from a storm. Very rarely, one may find an exit leading to [[Level Pi]] within a gas station.







=== Turbine fields: === The most common area within '''Level 241''' is the turbine field, which appears as grassy areas that stretch out infinitely with large clusters of wind turbines. Sometimes, though very rarely, one may encounter a fallen turbine that has not been repaired naturally by the level. It is advised to stay away from locations with fallen turbines, as this is an indicator that a '''False Turbine''' is nearby. They have a small chance of exploding as a result of power capacitors failingExplosions are rare, and typically only happen if a turbine hits the ground. Turbine fields are the only location where '''False Turbines''' can be found.
== Entities == '''Level 241''' is home to a level-exclusive entity, '''False Turbines''', along with [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], and, very rarely, [[Entity 5|Clumps]].
{{Entity Class|Agg=2|Int=4|Dan=5|Hab=Level 241|no=419}}
{{Blurimage|file=False turbine.jpg|caption=An image of a False turbine, taken from a safe distance. The photo was found in [[Level 4]].|align=left}} The '''False Turbine''' is an entity exclusive to '''Level 241'''. Operations conducted by the [[XONE Laboratories|XONE]] revealed the presence of multiple other entities within Level 241, leading to the discovery and documentation of False Turbines. It appears as a black wind turbine only found within the turbine fields. In addition, they are considerably larger and have longer blades compared to a normal turbine. '''False turbines''' can move but do so only when unobserved. They are typically easy to avoid, as constant eye contact will render them harmless. If a wanderer is to approach one, it will attempt to grab the wanderer with several tendrils that extend from the base of the entity. Once the victim is seized, they are lifted into the air where they will be sliced by the blades. The tendrils will retract once the wanderer is about 500 meters away. False Turbines can also blow hallucinogenic gases into the paths of wanderers, causing them to see objects, entities, or dangers that do not exist. The hallucinations will attempt to guide the wanderer closer toward the entity. Unfortunately, the gas is colorless and is extremely difficult to identify before it is inhaled. Minutes before the sequence of hallucinations begins, the wanderer will experience headaches and will find it hard to think straight. It is heavily advised to drink [[Object 1|Almond Water]] to combat the effects of the hallucinogenic gas and move as far away from the False Turbines as possible before the gases affect the brain, starting the hallucinations. The biology of the False Turbines is unnatural. The way the entity obtains sustenance is by feeding off nutrients in the ground with the tendrils or by capturing wanderers to eat. False Turbines have no sense of sight or smell, only relying on openings in their base to listen to the environment. It is believed that they are able to mimic other objects to blend in with the surroundings. However, the only form they have currently grown into is a turbine. It is unknown why this is the case.
== Colonies and Outposts == As a result of the [[Phenomenon 15|isolation effect]], no bases or outposts have been established within the level. == Events ==
===
'''The Wrath of the Elements'''
=== {{Blurimage|file=Flooded hoose.png|caption=A photograph of a flood, taken during The Wrath of the Elements}}'''Level 241''' is under the heavy influence of [[Phenomenon 106|Belphegor's Prime]], which is the reason behind most of the disastrous events that occur within the level, including the devastating event known as the Wrath of the Elements. The Wrath of the Elements will sometimes cause major storms to occur across the level, forming tornadoes as the wind speed increases to unprecedented levels. Other times, the event will inexplicably cause all cameras within the level to malfunction, and any photos taken inside the level will be corrupted until the wanderer leaves the level. In both cases, there will be a massive increase in wind speed, and a heavy increase of False Turbines will populate the level. Serious flooding is known to commonly occur during this event, with the water level reaching the top of certain hills, resulting in many deaths by drowning. Temperatures may drop to staggering levels, allowing the possibility of a deadly rain of hail during that period of time. It is believed that in the past, a severe occurrence of [[Phenomenon 106]] was the cause behind the frequent formation of tornadoes and appearances of False Turbines in the level now.
===
Recovered Notes
=== {{Underline|FFFFFF}}
'''Turbine Fields'''

Recovered note of Level 241 by Tommy W.
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
I remember it was 1991, right at the fall of the Soviet Union, when I was 3 years old, something happened, something strange. Well, what I saw scared me a lot. The Wrath is what they call it. It felt like I was in a horrible storm... Wind turbines loomed over me in all directions, and then I saw a slightly discolored turbine with tentacles approach me. I was scared out of my wits. Then I woke up. Then, many years later, I recall that I was visiting my grandparents' farm. The weather was perfect until the skies darkened and a tornado rolled in from the west. I quickly led my grandparents into the sturdiest shed I could find on the farm. I quickly went to find another hiding spot—the shed only had space for the both of them. I ran towards the barn. My foot hit a rock, and I fell, blacking out. When I came to, I was utterly confused; my grandparents' farm was nowhere in sight. All that was left were open fields in all directions. Everything seemed eerily familiar, those turbines and the very grass that I was stepping on. I thought it was just a dream; I slapped myself, but I didn't wake up. Denial set in, then left after a while. I realized I was stuck in this place, a hell of some sort. I took shelter in a shed and started writing this text. I just wish that I could wake up and have this all be a dream. ''It wouldn't be the first time.''
[[File:RAINP0001.webp|center|thumb|619x619px]]
''- Tommy W., 09/07/2008''
----
=== Timeline === {{Blurimage|Caption=|caption=An image of Level 241 during phase 4|file=TornadIn241.jpg}} * Phase 1 (0 seconds): The wanderer should feel an increase in wind speed. The increase should range from 0.2 kph to 3 kph. False turbines will be seen more frequently. Any and all exits will close, making it impossible to exit the level. * Phase 2 (1 minute): Storm clouds begin to form, typically those of a supercell. Along with the approaching storm, rain should begin to fall from the clouds already present in the sky, which will turn dark gray and expand. It is recommended to find shelter during this stage. * Phase 3 (5 minutes): At this point, the wind will reach around 100 kph, and tornadoes will begin to form. Any wanderer outside a shelter at this time must find a way indoors or risk death. * Phase 4 (30 minutes): Several turbines will have fallen at this point due to the strong, 151 kph winds ravaging the level. Most high-rise or weak structures within the level at the time will be blown away, hit by a tornado, or destroyed by a falling turbine. * Phase 5 (1 hour): Around this time, the winds will have gotten to a strength of 250 kph, the climax of the event. Any tornadoes within the level at the time will become at least EF5 intensity. Once this phase has occurred, the storm will begin to dissipate. * Phase 6 (1 hour & 30 minutes): The storm will begin to die down, and all exits will open up again, allowing one to leave the level. Wind speed will have reached 50 kph. * Phase 7 (1 hour and 45 minutes): The wind speed will have returned to normal, and the level will fully repair itself. False turbines will still be in abundance, however.
== Colonies & Outposts == Due to the [[Phenomenon 15|isolation effect]] within the level, no colonies or outposts can be established. == Entrances == You no-clip into a rusted wind turbine and find solace, a refuge from [[Level 41]] in the form of rolling hills. As you wander through [[Level 10.4]], the grass morphs into a shade of brilliant green as you enter '''Level 241'''. The exit sign named "Exit 241" shimmers in the moonlight. As you leave [[Level 690]], you realize there is yet another highway waiting on the other side, this time in a different place. After running through halls of monotony and confusion within [[Level 27]], you see a hole in the wall. You fit through the wooden wall and see a large pasture. Deep in [[Level 620|Level 620,]] the elevator labelled "Surface Power" cranks to life as the salty atmosphere changes into a bright one as you find yourself exiting into a shed. The glass of [[Level 543]] splinters into pieces as you jump through the window. You begin to fall rapidly from the sky, landing safely in a tranquil field with turbines all around you. == Exits == After driving for hours, you notice the highways around you [[Level 690|multiply in all directions]] A sharp contrast to the green fields around you catches your eye. You see a farm appear before you. Crossing the fence, the farm around you expands and morphs into the unmistakable terrain of [[Level 10]]. You notice a subtle shift in your surroundings as you submerge yourself in a pool of Almond Water. As you resurface, you feel a great sense of relief, knowing you have been transported to a paradise, the serene waters of [[Level 37.2]]. As you enter a storm cellar to seek safety from the storm, you realize the cellars are reminiscent of one from a [[Level 9.99|lightning-stricken hellscape]]. Suddenly, your vision shifts as the [[Level Pi|saturation]] of the nearby hills changes. You enter the brutalist building, only to turn around and see it [[Level 242|multiplying all around you]] {{Redirect|Prev=240|Next=242}}{{Footnotes}}
'''> Open Credits'''
